description: This is a traditional org theory course applied to education.
notes: This course mixed K-12, Higher Ed, PhD, and EdD students and was a bit tough to teach for that reason. I welcome feedback on how it could be improved!

description: This is a course for EdD students that provides a variety of theories of organization that I hope will be useful to them in their dissertations in practice.

department: School of Education and Human Development — Administration & Supervision Program Area
description: Developing High-performance Organizations is a doctoral-level course for ExSEL students and others interested in understanding how organizations evolve, develop, improve, and decline. Students examine organizational theories, case studies, research on efforts to improve organizational performance, and methods for studying organizations over extended periods of time.

courseTitle: Organizational Theory in Education and Other Social Services
description: This course integrates perspectives from organizational theory, sociology, and psychology to understand how schools function as complex social organizations. It draws on classic and contemporary studies to help students analyze, understand, and formulate responses to problems, dilemmas, and changes in schools and other social services. The course studies how organizational structure, culture, network, and interventions impact practices and outcomes.

Tools for Organizational Analysis is a collaborative course designed to help students harness the fundamentals of organizational analysis to address key questions about organizational behavior and performance. We pay special attention to research design and methodologies that address the dynamics of stability and change at the field and organizational levels.

courseTitle: Topics in Organizations, Institutions, and Society: Persistence and Change Among Public, Private, and Non-Profit Settings
description: The course is organized as a seminar and will blend foundational studies in institutional theory with contemporary work from sociology, organization sciences, management, education, and nonprofit studies. An overarching theme of the course addresses how new practices and organizational forms spread (diffusion), how they become taken for granted or deeply embedded in social life (institutionalization), and when or how they change or are replaced.
This course builds on a first undergraduate course in org theory that uses Bolman and Deal to help students apply org theory to contemporary organizational issues.
notes: This course is taught differently by different instructors. Gareth Jones' textbook was formally used and is the business-version of org theory.

description: A PhD seminar on organizational theory for students in the school of education.
Studies in Organizational Change is an advanced course in the Learning & Organizational Change major designed to introduce key constructs and ideas about change in organizations.

courseTitle: UEL 9012: Contexts of Educational Systems: Organizational Theory in Education
description: In this course, we dig deeply into one area of scholarship that may be particularly helpful to scholar-practitioners: organizational theory. This course is an introduction to organizational theory in education, with the unique lens of using organizational theory to solve problems of practice.

courseTitle: Lenses for Understanding and Examining K-12 Policy Implementation
description: The purpose of this seminar course is to familiarize students with organizational theory and its applications to K-12 policy implementation.

courseTitle: Lenses for Understanding and Examining K-12 Policy Implementation
description: The purpose of this seminar course is to familiarize students with organizational theory and its applications to K-12 policy implementation.
